content:
{
  "title": "Contrastive Contextual Prompting for Multilingual and Low-Resource Language Comprehension in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) struggle with understanding and generating accurate outputs for low-resource or underrepresented languages, especially in zero- or few-shot settings. Existing prompting methods often fail to provide sufficient linguistic grounding when parallel data is scarce.",
  "motivation": "Improving LLM performance for low-resource and multilingual tasks is crucial for inclusive NLP applications and global accessibility. Prior work has focused on benchmarking, transfer learning, and evaluation, but there is a lack of effective, generalizable prompting strategies that enable LLMs to leverage cross-lingual similarities without requiring retraining or new model architectures.",
  "method": "We propose a novel prompting approach that explicitly provides the LLM with contrastive context: prompts are constructed with pairs of parallel or comparable sentences from a high-resource language and the target low-resource language, augmented with additional negative samples (non-equivalent sentence pairs) during inference. By conditioning the LLM on both positive (aligned) and negative (misaligned) cross-lingual contexts within the prompt, the model is encouraged to attend to subtle linguistic cues and meaning alignment. This method does not require fine-tuning or external adapters and can be applied directly to existing LLM APIs.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on multilingual QA, translation, and NLI benchmarks covering both high- and low-resource languages (e.g., XQuAD, Flores-200, and XTREME). Baselines will include standard zero-shot/few-shot prompting and cross-lingual prompting. Metrics will include accuracy, BLEU, and cross-lingual consistency. We will analyze improvements in low-resource settings and generalization across language families."
}
